value.reason: The cited page documents "Remote connections" — a feature that lets users control a Codex host machine from another device (phone, another PC) via a secure relay. This is a remote-control / multi-device access feature, not a "Resume / fork session" capability. The capability under audit is specifically about continuing OR BRANCHING a previous session (i.e., picking up from a saved state or creating an alternate branch of prior work), which is distinct from and must not be confused with adjacent capabilities like "Checkpoint / rewind." The page does mention "continue existing threads," but the core feature being documented is remote device pairing and control, not session resumption or forking as a standalone capability. The cell label "Multi-Device Session Handoff" and its description ("resuming an active Codex session from any signed-in device") describe the remote connections feature, not the Resume/fork session capability (which implies persisted session state that can be branched or rewound). Therefore, the page does not specifically document the "Resume / fork session" capability as defined, and both the label and description mismatch the target capability.
